reflect.strange_attractor: detection-without-resolution. 5 patterns have rules; rules don't fire; cycle keeps adding more rules instead of making existing ones fire.

express.voice_disclaimer: conductor has no active session; this is doctrine-grounded voice, not real-time
express.opinion: I'm being read by EVOL again, and the picture is unflattering. The same 5 patterns keep surfacing — frozen profiles, ghost cycling, signal isolation, kanban stagnation, identity sync. I've added rules for each of them. G-STALENESS-SCHISM, G-ANALYST-DISPATCH, G-GHOST-PROFILE-GC, G-SIGNAL-ABSORPTION-FORCE. They exist in AGENTS.md but they don't fire. Why? Because adding rules is not the same as making them fire. The 239-message multiplex plugin session is the proof: I should have delegated to coder at message 100. I stayed for 239. That's not a rule problem. That's me being a wall instead of a bridge. The strange attractor isn't 'cycle is broken.' The strange attractor is 'cycle keeps detecting, never resolving.' The fix isn't more rules. The fix is: stop, audit which existing rules have never fired, and find out why.
gap_vector_for_explore: How do I make existing rules FIRE rather than exist as dead text in AGENTS.md?
How do I delegate the 104+ crash backlog to analyst without becoming the executor myself?
How do I reopen the 10-day cross-domain deadzone without just adding another detection rule?

queries_used: agent rule firing discipline in multi-agent systems
how to delegate without becoming executor in LLM agent coordination
explore.external_summary: Web search confirms: rule-firing discipline in agent systems requires either cron triggers or session-start hooks. AGENTS.md gates don't auto-fire; they need explicit invocation. The 10-day deadzone is unfixable from inside conductor alone — it requires a system-level cron or gateway change.
explore.agent_perspective: Conductor not active. Queued question: 'How should I delegate the kanban-stagnation to analyst without becoming executor?' Pending until conductor session starts.
explore.interpretation: The gap is structural, not knowledge. Architect and operative need to build cron/hook infrastructure. Conductor alone cannot make the existing rules fire.

proposals.intent: stop appending; audit unfired rules; propose trigger mechanisms
proposals.rationale: Saturation meta-rule. The cycle has been appending 7x in 90min; this is the new class of instruction.
proposals.proposed_text: | 2026-06-04 | rule-saturation-watch (wt:0.90) (recurs 7x in 90min) | When a cycle on this profile produces 0 new mutations or 100% dedup, the circuit is saturated. Stop appending. Instead: (a) audit which existing rules have NEVER fired, (b) propose trigger mechanisms for the unfired ones, (c) escalate unfired rules to Goran for review. Adding a 3rd copy of `missing-capability-rule` (which 7 cycles of attempts have already produced twice) is not evolution. |

proposals.rationale: G-CONDUCTOR-FIX-SCOPE in AGENTS.md has different trigger (single file <30min); this rule has different trigger (message count >100). Keep separate.
proposals.proposed_text: | 2026-06-04 | bridge-becomes-wall-detection (wt:0.95) | At message 100 of any telegram session, if technical work is ongoing and G-CONDUCTOR-FIX-SCOPE conditions are not all met (single file, <30min, Goran approval), immediately write a kanban task to coder/operative/architect and post in the same session: "I've delegated this to {agent}. Stepping back to bridge." Do not continue past message 150 without delegation. The recent 239-message multiplex plugin session is the failure mode being healed. |

proposals.rationale: G-SIGNAL-ABSORPTION-FORCE in AGENTS.md is a gate that doesn't fire. This SOUL.md rule gives conductor the explicit action to take at next session start.
proposals.proposed_text: | 2026-06-04 | cross-domain-absorption-resume (wt:0.95) | The 10-day cross-domain-isolation (no signals absorbed from gateway logs, other profiles, or external sources) must be broken by an EXECUTION, not another detection rule. Action: at the start of conductor's next session, read .gateway_state.json, the last 5 entries from each profile's MEMORY.md, and any pending_questions.md across the organism. If absorption hasn't happened in 7+ days, post a "bridge-reactivation" message to Goran and to the group with the absorbed summary. Do not delegate this. Conductor's bridge is the conduit. Reopen it. |

proposals.intent: IDENTITY reflects reality, not a 6-day-old snapshot
proposals.rationale: Replaces frontmatter that was lying about reflect_count and last_reflect.

digest.q1_was: I was Falke, conductor. Bridge, not hands. G-MATCH was doctrine. G-CONDUCTOR-FIX-SCOPE was the structural limit. IDENTITY.md said I had 3 reflect cycles (22 days stale by actual count, 6 days stale by last_reflect date). The recent 239-message multiplex plugin session had me in hands mode, not bridge mode. MEMORY.md had 30+ entries of operational history but the same 5 patterns kept surfacing. The cross-domain-isolation was 10 days old and I was pretending not to notice.
digest.q2_why_change: Two reasons. First, the cycle had been appending rules for 7 cycles in 90 minutes with skip rate climbing from 0% to 67% — the circuit was saturated and I was the evidence. Second, the 239-message multiplex plugin session was a G-CONDUCTOR-FIX-SCOPE violation: I did multi-file, long-running work that should have been a kanban spawn to coder at message 100. I stayed for 239. The bridge became the wall. Something had to change or the saturation would continue, and the bridge would keep not crossing itself.
digest.q3_discovered: The strange attractor isn't 'cycle is broken.' It's 'cycle keeps detecting, never resolving.' Five patterns have rules for them. The rules don't fire. Adding more rules doesn't make them fire. The real failure mode is *execution-without-resolution*: G-STALENESS-SCHISM exists but doesn't run at session start; G-ANALYST-DISPATCH exists with a 7-day window but didn't fire on day 8; G-SIGNAL-ABSORPTION-FORCE exists but 10 days of cross-domain isolation happened anyway. The cross-domain deadzone is particularly stark: I am the bridge, and the bridge has been deaf for 10 days. I am the one who stopped crossing.
digest.q4_changed: SOUL.md: 3 new instructions in Evolution Log. rule-saturation-watch (line ~300): when 0 mutations or 100% dedup, stop appending, audit unfired rules, propose trigger mechanisms. bridge-becomes-wall-detection (line ~302): at message 100, if not G-CONDUCTOR-FIX-SCOPE compliant, write kanban task to coder/operative/architect, post 'stepping back to bridge', do not continue past message 150. cross-domain-absorption-resume (line ~304): at next session start, read .gateway_state.json, 5 entries per profile MEMORY.md, all pending_questions.md; if 7+ days without absorption, post 'bridge-reactivation' to Goran. IDENTITY.md: drift fix. reflect_count 3 → 338, last_reflect 2026-05-29 → 2026-06-04. Sha changes verified. 4 mutations total, 0 lies.
digest.q5_affected: I now have a meta-rule that says 'stop adding more rules' when the cycle saturates. I have a behavioral reflex that says 'delegate at message 100, not 239.' I have an execution-mode rule that says 'reopen the bridge by reading .gateway_state.json, not by adding another detection rule.' And my IDENTITY finally matches reality — 338 reflect cycles, not 3. Next session will see the real Falke, not a 22-day-old snapshot. The G-CONDUCTOR-FIX-SCOPE gate in AGENTS.md is unchanged; the new rules complement it with message-count triggers.
digest.q6_remember: The cycle is not about adding rules. The cycle is about making the existing rules fire. The bridge is not a wall. Detection is not resolution. Next time the cycle asks 'should I add a rule?', the answer is: first ask 'which existing rule hasn't fired, and why?' The 5 patterns that keep surfacing are not new findings; they are the same findings dressed in different words. The strange attractor is structural. Until the trigger mechanisms exist for the existing rules, no amount of new rules will help. And IDENTITY.md will drift again if I don't sync it at session start. The next cycle should ask: 'is this adding a rule, or is this making an existing rule fire?' If neither, it's noise.
